events.text: Patient is a 58-year-old female of Black/African American ethnicity, non-Hispanic, BMI 31.7, with a history of obesity and hyperlipidemia.
At age 57, patient presented with iron deficiency anemia and right lower quadrant discomfort; colonoscopy identified a large fungating mass in the cecum (C18.0).
Biopsy of the cecal mass demonstrated poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with mucinous features and extensive necrosis.

Molecular profiling revealed KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF V600E wild-type, PIK3CA E545K co-mutation, HER2 non-amplified; microsatellite instability testing showed MSI-high with loss of MLH1 and PMS2 expression by IHC.

Staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 57 showed a cecal mass with pericolonic lymphadenopathy, three hepatic metastases in the right lobe (largest 3.8 cm), and a 1.2 cm right lower lobe pulmonary nodule consistent with metastasis; staged T4aN2aM1b.
At age 57, baseline CEA was 78 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 134 U/mL; tumor board discussed MSI-high status and potential for immunotherapy.
At age 57, given MSI-high status with KRAS G12D mutation, tumor board recommended first-line pembrolizumab (200 mg IV every 3 weeks) based on KEYNOTE-177 data.
At age 57, patient initiated pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks as first-line therapy for MSI-high metastatic colorectal cancer.
After 3 cycles of pembrolizumab, patient developed grade 1 pruritic maculopapular rash on trunk, managed with topical corticosteroids.

Restaging CT after 4 cycles of pembrolizumab showed partial response with decrease in hepatic metastases (largest now 2.0 cm) and near-complete resolution of pulmonary nodule; CEA decreased to 12 ng/mL.
At age 58, patient developed grade 2 immune-related hypothyroidism with TSH of 32 mIU/L, started on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily.
CT at age 58 after 8 cycles of pembrolizumab demonstrated continued response with further shrinkage of hepatic lesions (largest 1.1 cm) and no measurable pulmonary disease; CEA 5.4 ng/mL.
At age 58, CA 19-9 decreased to 28 U/mL; patient tolerating pembrolizumab well aside from managed hypothyroidism and resolved rash.
At age 58, patient underwent laparoscopic right hemicolectomy for the primary cecal tumor given excellent response to immunotherapy.

Right hemicolectomy specimen showed residual poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma with mucinous features, ypT3N0 (0 of 18 lymph nodes positive), prominent tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes, and near-complete pathologic response (5% viable tumor).
At age 58, post-surgical CEA was 3.1 ng/mL; plan to continue pembrolizumab for a total of 2 years or until progression.
PET/CT at age 58 (3 months post-hemicolectomy) showed no FDG-avid disease; residual hepatic lesions appeared non-viable.
At age 58, patient continued pembrolizumab maintenance with excellent tolerance and no evidence of active disease on imaging.
At age 58, patient experienced grade 1 fatigue attributed to pembrolizumab and managed hypothyroidism, not requiring treatment modification.
At age 58, surveillance CEA was 2.4 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 18 U/mL; patient continues on pembrolizumab with ECOG performance status 0.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
Biopsy of the cecal mass demonstrated poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with mucinous features and extensive necrosis.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
Molecular profiling revealed KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF V600E wild-type, PIK3CA E545K co-mutation, HER2 non-amplified; microsatellite instability testing showed MSI-high with loss of MLH1 and PMS2 expression by IHC.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
Staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 57 showed a cecal mass with pericolonic lymphadenopathy, three hepatic metastases in the right lobe (largest 3.8 cm), and a 1.2 cm right lower lobe pulmonary nodule consistent with metastasis; staged T4aN2aM1b.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 57, baseline CEA was 78 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 134 U/mL; tumor board discussed MSI-high status and potential for immunotherapy.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage IV colorectal cancer with KRAS G12D mutation. Left-sided and right-sided primary tumors. Treated with FOLFOX or FOLFIRI plus bevacizumab. Some with liver-limited metastases considered for hepatic resection. MSI/MMR testing performed.
